method_atom: a framework for characterising, comparing and classifying the three-point (bispectral) statistics of a random field by defining an inner-product correlator between candidate three-point functions, computing their cross-correlations to measure mutual distinguishability, and applying an eigenmode/basis decomposition to group signatures into distinct separable classes and quantify their independence. It includes efficient techniques (approximate projection geometry and cubic interpolation) for evaluating a general non-separable three-point function that arises after linearly transforming an underlying source field, plus a correlator-based normalisation so amplitude estimates from different models are consistently comparable.
object_atom: primordial non-Gaussianity of the early-universe density perturbations from inflation as imprinted on the cosmic microwave background, i.e. the various theoretical primordial bispectrum models (equilateral, local, warm, flat, feature) and their induced CMB angular bispectrum with the f_NL amplitude parameter.
brief_reason: The transferable part is the correlator/cross-correlation and eigenmode machinery for classifying and comparing higher-order statistical signatures of a transformed random field; the domain-specific part is inflationary primordial non-Gaussianity observed through the CMB bispectrum.
